routes::canary::CanaryState {\n203\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n204\t        // Canary routes require Redis task store\n205\t        let redis_store = }\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\t#[tokio::main]\n216\tasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n217\t    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n218\t    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n219\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n220\t\n221\t    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n222\t    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n226\t\n227\t    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n228\t\n229\t    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n230\t    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n231\t    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n232\t    if let Some(otel_layer) = .with_target(true)\n237\t            .with_current_span(true)\n238\t            .with_span_list(false);\n239\t        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n240\t        registry()\n241\t            .with(otel_layer)\n242\t            .with(filter)\n243\t            .with(json_layer)\n244\t            .init();\n245\t    } else {\n246\t        let json_layer = .with_span_list(false);\n252\t        registry()\n253\t            .with(filter)\n254\t            .with(json_layer)\n255\t            .init();\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n259\t    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n260\t    // left — its fields propagate to all child spans and events.\n261\t    let _pod_span = tracing::info_span!(\"runtime\", pod_id = %pod_id).entered();\n262\t\n263\t    info!(\n264\t        shards = config.shards,\n265\t        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n266\t        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n267\t        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n268\t    );\n269\t\n270\t    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n271\t    // start the search UI without it\").\n272\t    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n273\t        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n274\t        match  }\n283\t        }\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    // Build unified state\n287\t    let state = UnifiedState::new(config.clone());\n288\t\n289\t    // Start health checker background task\n290\t    let background task (plan §4)\n296\t    if let Some(ref worker) = state.admin.rebalancer_worker {\n297\t        let worker = worker.clone();\n298\t        let pod_id = state.pod_id.clone();\n299\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n300\t            info!(\n301\t                pod_id = %pod_id,\n302\t                \"rebalancer worker task starting\"\n303\t            );\n304\t            // Load any state.pod_id.clone(),\n321\t        };\n322\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n323\t            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n324\t        });\n325\t\n326\t        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n327\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n328\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n329\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n330\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n331\t        let            move |session_id: String| {\n341\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n342\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n343\t                },\n344\t            )\n345\t            .await\n346\t            {\n347\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n348\t            }\n349\t        });\n350\t    }\n351\t\n352\t    // Start drift reconciler background task (plan §13.5)\n353\t    // Always runs but uses Mode B leader election for horizontal scaling\n354\t    if let Some(ref redis) =         node_addresses: config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect(),\n361\t            leader_scope: \"drift_reconciler\".to_string(),\n362\t            pod_id: pod_id.clone(),\n363\t         }\n371\t        );\n372\t\n373\t        let drift_reconciler = miroir_core::drift_reconciler::DriftReconciler::with_metrics(\n374\t            drift_config,\n375\t    // The handle is dropped here only on process exit\n396\t            info!(\"task registry TTL pruner started\");\n397\t            // Keep this task alive forever\n398\t            std::future::pending::<()>().await;\n399\t        });\n400\t    } else {\n401\t        info!(\"task registry TTL pruner not available (no task store)\");\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    // Start canary config values for the search executor\n412\t            let search_config = config.clone();\n413\t            let search_executor: miroir_core::canary::SearchExecutor = Arc::new(\n414\t                move |index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery| -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = miroir_core::error::Result<SearchResponse>> + Send>> {\n415\t                    let index_uid = index_uid.to_string();\n416\t                    let query = query.clone();\n417\t                    let config = search_config.clone();\n418\t\n419\t                    Box::pin(async move {\n420\t                        // For canary queries, we execute against the first available healthy node\n421\t                        let node_addresses: Vec<_> = config.nodes.iter()\n422\t                            .map(|n| n.address.clone())\n423\t                            .collect();\n424\t\n425\t                        for address in node_addresses {\n426\t                            let client =                           {\n430\t                                Ok(c) => c,\n431\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n432\t                            };\n433\t\n434\t                            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n435\t\n436\t                            // Build the search request body\n437\t                            let mut body = match serde_json::to_value(&query) {\n438\t                                Ok(v) => v,\n439\t                                Err(e) => return large responses for canary queries\n443\t                            if !body.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).is_some() {\n444\t                                body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(20);\n445\t                            }\n446\t\n447\t                            let response = match client.post(&url)\n448\t                                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", config.node_master_key))\n449\t                                .json(&body)\n450\t                                .send()\n451\t                                .await\n452\t                            {\n453\t                                Ok(r) => r,\n454\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n455\t                            };\n456\t\n457\t                            if response.status().is_success() {\n458\t                                if let Ok(text) = response.text().await {\n459\t Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::Topology(\n468\t                            \"All nodes failed for canary query\".to_string()\n469\t                        ))\n470\t                    })\n471\t                }\n472\t            );\n473\t\n474\t            // Create metrics emitter callback\n475\t            let metrics_for_canary = state.metrics.clone();\n476\t            let CanaryStatus::Error => \"error\",\n483\t                    };\n484\t                    metrics_for_canary.inc_canary_runs(&result.canary_id, result_str);\n485\t                    metrics_for_canary.observe_canary_latency_ms(&result.canary_id, result.latency_ms as f64);\n486\t\n487\t                    for failure in &result.failed_assertions {\n488\t            }\n511\t                        }\n512\t                    }\n513\t                    min_version\n514\t                }\n515\t            );\n516\t\n517\t            // Create and start .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n544\t        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n545\t        .nest(\"/indexes\", Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n551\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n552\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n553\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n554\t        // 3.      //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n558\t        //\n559\t        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n560\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n561\t            TelemetryState {\n562\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n563\t                pod_id: middleware::request_id_middleware,\n569\t        ))\n570\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n571\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n572\t        ))\n573\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n574\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n575\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n576\t            state.auth.clone(),\n577\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n578\t        ))\n579\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n580\t            auth::CsrfState {\n581\t .with_state(state.clone());\n587\t\n588\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n589\t        .parse()\n590\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n591\t    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n592\t\n593\t    info!(\n594\t        main_addr = shutdown\n604\t    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n605\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n606\t\n607\t    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n608\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n609\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n610\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n611\t\n612\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n613\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n614\t        main_server,\n615\t        metrics_server\n616\t    );\n617\t\n618\t    // Check for errors\n619\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n620\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n621\t    }\n622\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n623\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n624\t    }\n625\t\n626\t    Ok(())\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n630\t///\n631\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n632\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n633\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n634\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n635\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n636\t    ));\n637\t\n638\t    loop {\n639\t        interval.tick().await;\n640\t\n641\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n642\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n643\t\n644\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n645\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n646\t\n647\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n648\t            // Get current node status\n649\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n650\t\n651\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n652\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n653\t                continue;\n654\t            }\n655\t\n656\t            // Get node address\n657\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n658\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n659\t                None => {\n660\t                    all_healthy = false;\n661\t                    continue;\n662\t                }\n663\t            };\n664\t\n665\t            // Try to reach the node\n666\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n667\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n668\t                .build()\n669\t            {\n670\t                Ok(c) => c,\n671\t                Err(_) => {\n672\t                    all_healthy = false;\n673\t                    continue;\n674\t                }\n675\t            };\n676\t\n677\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n678\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n679\t\n680\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n681\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n682\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n683\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n684\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n685\t                }\n686\t            } else {\n687\t                all_healthy = false;\n688\t            }\n689\t        }\n690\t\n691\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n692\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n693\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n694\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n695\t        }\n696\t\n697\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n698\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n699\t\n700\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n701\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n702\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n703\t\n704\t        // Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus\n705\t        state.sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus().await;\n706\t\n707\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n708\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n709\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n710\t        }\n711\t\n712\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n713\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n714\t    }\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n718\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n719\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n720\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n721\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n722\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n723\t\n724\t    // Per-node shard count\n725\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n726\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n727\t\n728\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n729\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n730\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n731\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n732\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n733\t            );\n734\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n735\t                let healthy = node_map\n736\t                    .get(node_id)\n737\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n738\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n739\t                if healthy {\n740\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n741\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n742\t                }\n743\t            }\n744\t        }\n745\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n746\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n747\t        } else {\n748\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n749\t        }\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n753\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n754\t    } else {\n755\t        1.0\n756\t    };\n757\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n758\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n759\t\n760\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n761\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n762\t    }\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n766\t///\n767\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n768\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n769\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n770\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n771\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n772\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n773\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n774\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n775\t\n776\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n777\t        if max > 0 {\n778\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n779\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n780\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n781\t        }\n782\t    }\n783\t\n784\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n785\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n786\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n787\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n788\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n789\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n790\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n791\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n792\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n793\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n794\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n795\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n796\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n797\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n798\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n799\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n800\t                }\n801\t            }\n802\t        }\n803\t    }\n804\t\n805\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n806\t    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n807\t    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n808\t    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n809\t    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n810\t    metrics.set_leader(true);\n811\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n815\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n816\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n817\t        .ok()\n818\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n819\t}\n820\t\n821\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n822\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n823\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n824\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n825\t            .await\n826\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n827\t    };\n828\t\n829\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n830\t    let terminate = async {\n831\t        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n832\t            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n833\t            .recv()\n834\t            .await;\n835\t    };\n836\t\n837\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n838\t    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n839\t\n840\t    tokio::select! {\n841\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n842\t        _ = terminate => {},\n843\t    }\n844\t\n845\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n846\t\n847\t    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n848\t    otel::shutdown_otel();\n849\t}\n850\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_3c3143cab492475487a4ee6a","session_id":"ea878048-2e42-4b12-a662-8906952566a7","uuid":"1c1bbee9-70d8-41aa-a6f6-a0f85ced3e0d","timestamp":"2026-05-22T19:29:38.835Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4697b349955341e798a4ed99","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n4\t//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n5\t//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n8\t//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n9\t//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n10\t//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n11\t\n12\tuse axum::{\n13\t    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n14\t    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n15\t    middleware::Next,\n16\t    response::{IntoResponse, subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n28\t\n29\tuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n30\t\n31\ttype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n32\t\n33\t/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\t/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct JwtClaims {\n52\t    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n53\t    pub sub: String,\n54\t    /// Index this token grants access to.\n55\t    pub idx: String,\n56\t    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n57\t    pub scope: String,\n58\t    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n59\t    pub iat: u64,\n60\t    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n61\t    pub exp: u64,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\n65\tconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\n66\tconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n67\t\n68\t/// JWT header (always HS256).\n69\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n70\tstruct JwtHeader {\n71\t    alg: String,\n72\t    kid: String,\n73\t    typ: String,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77\t// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n78\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79\t\n80\t/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\n81\tfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n87\t\n88\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n89\t\n90\t    let mut mac =\n91\t        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n92\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n93\t    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n94\t    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n121\t    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n122\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n123\t    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n124\t\n125\t    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n126\t        .decode(parts[2])\n127\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n128\t\n129\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n204\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n205\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n206\t        };\n207\t        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n242\t    Malformed,\n243\t    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n244\t    InvalidSignature,\n245\t    /// Token has expired.\n246\t    Expired,\n247\t    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n248\t    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n256\t/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\n257\tpub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n258\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n259\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n260\t    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\n264\tpub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n265\t    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\n269\tpub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n270\t    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n274\t/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\n275\tpub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n276\t    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n277\t        Ok(())\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// Origin validation (plan §9)\n285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n286\t\n287\t/// Result of origin validation.\n288\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n289\tpub enum OriginVerdict {\n290\t    /// Origin is allowed.\n291\t    Allowed,\n292\t    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n293\t    Forbidden,\n294\t    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n295\t    Missing,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n299\t/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\n300\tpub fn validate_origin(\n301\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n302\t    allowed_origins: &[String],\n303\t    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n304\t) -> OriginVerdict {\n305\t    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n306\t    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n307\t\n308\t    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n309\t    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n310\t\n311\t    let provided_origin = match referer {\n312\t        Some(o) => o,\n313\t        None => {\n314\t            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n315\t            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n316\t                OriginVerdict::Missing\n317\t            } else {\n318\t                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n319\t            };\n320\t        }\n321\t    };\n322\t\n323\t    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n324\t    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n325\t        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n326\t            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n327\t            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n328\t                &ref_val[..idx]\n329\t            } else {\n330\t                ref_val\n331\t            }\n332\t        } else {\n333\t            provided_origin\n334\t        }\n335\t    } else {\n336\t        provided_origin\n337\t    };\n338\t\n339\t    // Check against allowed origins\n340\t    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n341\t        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n342\t        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n343\t            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n344\t                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n345\t                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n346\t                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n347\t                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n348\t                }\n349\t            }\n350\t        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n351\t            // Wildcard allows any origin\n352\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n353\t        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n354\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n362\t// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n363\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n364\t\n365\t/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n366\t/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\n367\tpub fn build_csp_header(\n368\t    base_template: &str,\n369\t    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n370\t) -> String {\n371\t    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n372\t        .split(';')\n373\t        .map(|s| s.trim())\n374\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n375\t        .map(|directive| {\n376\t            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n377\t            if parts.len() == 2 {\n378\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n379\t            } else {\n380\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n381\t            }\n382\t        })\n383\t        .collect();\n384\t\n385\t    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n386\t    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n387\t                      name: &str,\n388\t                      values: &[String]| {\n389\t        if values.is_empty() {\n390\t            return;\n391\t        }\n392\t        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n393\t        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n394\t            // Append to existing directive\n395\t            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n396\t            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n397\t        } else {\n398\t            // Add new directive\n399\t            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n400\t        }\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Merge each override category\n404\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n405\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n406\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n407\t\n408\t    // Rebuild CSP string\n409\t    directives\n410\t        .into_iter()\n411\t        .map(|(name, values)| {\n412\t            if values.is_empty() {\n413\t                name\n414\t            } else {\n415\t                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n416\t            }\n417\t        })\n418\t        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n419\t        .join(\"; \")\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n423\t// Dispatch verdict\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t\n426\t/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n427\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n428\tpub enum AuthVerdict {\n429\t    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n430\t    Exempt,\n431\t    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n432\t    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n433\t    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n434\t    JwtInvalid,\n435\t    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n436\t    JwtScopeDenied,\n437\t    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n438\t    InvalidAuth,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n443\tpub enum TokenKind {\n444\t    MasterKey,\n445\t    AdminKey,\n446\t    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n447\t    Jwt,\n448\t    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n449\t    AdminSession,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\timpl AuthVerdict {\n453\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n454\t        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n459\t// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n460\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n461\t\n462\t/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n463\t/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n464\t/// are never consulted.\n465\tpub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n466\t    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n467\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n468\t        return true;\n469\t    }\n470\t\n471\t    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n472\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n473\t        return true;\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n477\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n478\t        return true;\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n482\t    if method == Method::GET {\n483\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n484\t            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n485\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n490\t    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n491\t        return true;\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n495\t    if method == Method::GET {\n496\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n497\t            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n498\t            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n499\t                return true;\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n505\t    if method == Method::GET {\n506\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n507\t            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n508\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n509\t        }\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    false\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n516\t// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n517\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n518\t\n519\t/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n520\t/// dot-separated base64url segments).\n521\tpub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n522\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n523\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n524\t        return false;\n525\t    }\n526\t    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n527\t    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n528\t        !s.is_empty()\n529\t            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n530\t    })\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n534\t// Helper — admin path check\n535\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n536\t\n537\t/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\n538\tpub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n539\t    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n544\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n545\t\n546\t/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n547\t/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\n548\tpub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n549\t    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n553\t// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n554\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n555\t\n556\t/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n557\t///\n558\t/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n559\t/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\n560\tpub fn dispatch_bearer(\n561\t    method: &Method,\n562\t    path: &str,\n563\t    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n564\t    state: &AuthState,\n565\t) -> AuthVerdict {\n566\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n567\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n568\t        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    let token = match bearer_token {\n572\t        Some(t) => t,\n573\t        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n574\t    };\n575\t\n576\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n577\t    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n578\t        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n579\t            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n580\t            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n581\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n582\t            }\n583\t            Err(_) => {\n584\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n590\t    if is_admin_path(path) {\n591\t        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n592\t            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n593\t        }\n594\t        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n598\t    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n599\t        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n603\t    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n607\t// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n608\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n609\t\n610\t/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n611\t/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n612\t/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\n613\tpub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n614\t    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n615\t        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n616\t        None => false,\n617\t    }\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n621\t// Axum middleware\n622\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n623\t\n624\t/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\n625\tfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n626\t    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n627\t    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\n631\tpub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n632\t    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n633\t    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n634\t        let pair = pair.trim();\n635\t        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n636\t            return Some(value.to_string());\n637\t        }\n638\t    }\n639\t    None\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\n643\tpub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n644\t    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\n648\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n649\t    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n650\t    req: Request,\n651\t    next: Next,\n652\t) -> Response {\n653\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n654\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n655\t\n656\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n657\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n658\t        return next.run(req).await;\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n662\t    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n663\t        return next.run(req).await;\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n667\t    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n668\t    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n669\t    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n670\t        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n671\t            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n672\t                Ok(session_id) => {\n673\t                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n674\t                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n675\t                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n676\t                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n677\t                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n678\t                        )\n679\t                        .into_response();\n680\t                    }\n681\t                    let mut req = req;\n682\t                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n683\t                    return next.run(req).await;\n684\t                }\n685\t                Err(e) => {\n686\t                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n687\t                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n688\t                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n689\t                    tracing::warn!(\n690\t                        path = %path,\n691\t                        error = %e,\n692\t                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n693\t                    );\n694\t                }\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    // Extract bearer token\n700\t    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n701\t\n702\t    // Run the dispatch chain\n703\t    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n704\t\n705\t    match verdict {\n706\t        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n707\t        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n708\t            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n709\t            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n710\t        )\n711\t        .into_response(),\n712\t        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n713\t            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n714\t            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n715\t        )\n716\t        .into_response(),\n717\t        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n718\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n719\t            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n720\t        )\n721\t        .into_response(),\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n726\t// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n727\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n728\t\n729\t/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n730\t///\n731\t/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n732\t/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n733\t/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n734\t/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n735\t/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n736\t///\n737\t/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n738\t/// the token stored in the session.\n739\tpub async fn csrf_middleware(\n740\t    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n741\t    req: Request,\n742\t    next: Next,\n743\t) -> Response {\n744\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n745\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n746\t\n747\t    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n748\t    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n749\t        return next.run(req).await;\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n753\t    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n754\t        return next.run(req).await;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n758\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n759\t        return next.run(req).await;\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n763\t    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n764\t        return next.run(req).await;\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n768\t    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n769\t    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n770\t\n771\t    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n772\t    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n773\t        return next.run(req).await;\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    // Extract CSRF token from header\n777\t    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n778\t        Some(token) => token,\n779\t        None => {\n780\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n781\t                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n782\t                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n783\t            )\n784\t            .into_response();\n785\t        }\n786\t    };\n787\t\n788\t    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n789\t    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n790\t        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n791\t        None => {\n792\t            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n793\t            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n794\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n795\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n796\t                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n797\t            )\n798\t            .into_response();\n799\t        }\n800\t    };\n801\t\n802\t    // Validate CSRF token against session\n803\t    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n804\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n805\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n806\t            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n807\t        )\n808\t        .into_response();\n809\t    };\n810\t\n811\t    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n812\t        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n813\t        Ok(None) => {\n814\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n815\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n816\t                \"Admin session not found.\",\n817\t            )\n818\t            .into_response();\n819\t        }\n820\t        Err(e) => {\n821\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n822\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n823\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n824\t                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n825\t            )\n826\t            .into_response();\n827\t        }\n828\t    };\n829\t\n830\t    // Check if revoked\n831\t    if session.revoked {\n832\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n833\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n834\t            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n835\t        )\n836\t        .into_response();\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    // Check expiration\n840\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n841\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n842\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n843\t        .as_secs() as i64;\n844\t    if session.expires_at < now {\n845\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n847\t            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n848\t        )\n849\t        .into_response();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n853\t    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n854\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n855\t            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n856\t            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n857\t        )\n858\t        .into_response();\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    next.run(req).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n869\t/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n870\t/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n871\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n872\tpub enum RateLimitBucket {\n873\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n874\t    AdminLogin(String),\n875\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n876\t    SearchUi(String),\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\t/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n880\t/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n881\t/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n882\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n883\tpub struct RateLimiter;\n884\t\n885\timpl RateLimiter {\n886\t    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n887\t        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n888\t    }\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n892\t// Helpers\n893\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n894\t\n895\tfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n896\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n897\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n898\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n899\t        .as_secs()\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n903\t// Tests\n904\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n905\t\n906\t#[cfg(test)]\n907\tmod tests {\n908\t    use super::*;\n909\t\n910\t    fn test_key() -> SealKey {\n911\t        SealKey::from_bytes([42u8; 32])\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    fn test_state() -> AuthState {\n915\t        AuthState {\n916\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n917\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n918\t            jwt_primary: None,\n919\t            jwt_previous: None,\n920\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n921\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n922\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n923\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n924\t            ).unwrap(),\n925\t        }\n926\t    }\n927\t\n928\t    fn test_state_with_jwt() -> AuthState {\n929\t        AuthState {\n930\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n931\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n932\t            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n933\t            jwt_previous: None,\n934\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n935\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n936\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n937\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n938\t            ).unwrap(),\n939\t        }\n940\t    }\n941\t\n942\t    fn test_state_with_dual_jwt() -> AuthState {\n943\t        AuthState {\n944\t            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n945\t            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n946\t            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n947\t            jwt_previous: Some(\"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n948\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n949\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n950\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n951\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n952\t            ).unwrap(),\n953\t        }\n954\t    }\n955\t\n956\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n957\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt tests\n958\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n959\t\n960\t    #[test]\n961\t    fn get_metrics_requires_admin_key() {\n962\t        assert!(!is_dispatch_exempt(&Method::GET, {\n1265\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1266\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1267\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1268\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1269\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1270\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1271\t            iat: now - 3600,\n1272\t            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n1273\t        };\n1274\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1275\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1276\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n1277\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1278\t        };\n1279\t        let token = jwt_encode(\n1280\t            &header,\n1281\t            &claims,\n1282\t            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n1283\t        )\n1284\t        .unwrap();\n1285\t\n1286\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1287\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n1288\t    }\n1289\t\n1290\t    #[test]\n1291\t    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n1292\t        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n1293\t        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1294\t        // Tamper with the signature\n1295\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n1296\t        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n1297\t\n1298\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n1299\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n1300\t    }\n1301\t\n1302\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1303\t    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n1304\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1305\t\n1306\t    #[test]\n1307\t    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n1308\t        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n1309\t        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n1310\t\n1311\t        // Sign token with the previous secret\n1312\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n1313\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n1314\t            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n1315\t            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n1316\t            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n1317\t            iat: now,\n1318\t            exp: now + 900,\n1319\t        };\n1320\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1321\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1322\t            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n1323\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1324\t        };\n1325\t        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n1326\t\n1327\t        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n1328\t        let state = AuthState {\n1329\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1330\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1331\t            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n1332\t            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n1333\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1334\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1335\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1336\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1337\t            ).unwrap(),\n1338\t        };\n1339\t\n1340\t        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n1341\t        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&old_token).unwrap();\n1342\t        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user1\");\n1343\t\n1344\t        // And dispatch should authenticate it\n1345\t        let verdict = assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n1352\t    }\n1353\t\n1354\t    #[test]\n1355\t    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n1356\t        let state = fn rotation_wrong_secret_returns_invalid_signature() {\n1366\t        let state = AuthState {\n1367\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1368\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1369\t assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n1401\t    }\n1402\t\n1403\t    #[test]\n1404\t    fn leak_response_empty_previous_rejects_old_tokens() {\n1405\t        let state =          ).unwrap(),\n1415\t        };\n1416\t\n1417\t        let result = state.validate_jwt(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ0ZXN0In0.fake\");\n1418\t        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty));\n1419\t    }\n1420\t\n1421\t    #[test]\n1422\t    fn rotation_after_step5_steady_state_previous_removed() {\n1423\t        let primary = \"final-primary-secret-key-32bytes\";\n1424\t        let state = AuthState {\n1425\t            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n1426\t            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n1427\t            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n1428\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1429\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1430\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1431\t assert!(state.validate_jwt(&token).is_ok());\n1439\t\n1440\t        // Old tokens signed with now-removed previous fail\n1441\t        let old_claims = JwtClaims {\n1442\t            sub: epoch_seconds() + 800,\n1447\t        };\n1448\t        let old_header = JwtHeader {\n1449\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1450\t            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n1451\t \"old-previous-secret-now-removed\".as_bytes(),\n1457\t        )\n1458\t        .unwrap();\n1459\t        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&old_token).is_err());\n1460\t    }\n1461\t\n1462\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1463\t    // End-to-end rotation scenario\n1464\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1465\t\n1466\t    #[test]\n1467\t    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n1468\t        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1469\t        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1470\t\n1471\t        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n1472\t        let pre = AuthState {\n1473\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1474\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1475\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1476\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1477\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1478\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1479\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1480\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1481\t            ).unwrap(),\n1482\t        };\n1483\t        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1484\t        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1485\t\n1486\t        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n1487\t        let during = AuthState {\n1488\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1489\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1490\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1491\t            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1492\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1493\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1494\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1495\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1496\t            ).unwrap(),\n1497\t        };\n1498\t        // Old token still validates\n1499\t        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1500\t        // New tokens work too\n1501\t        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1502\t        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n1503\t\n1504\t        // Post-rotation: only v2\n1505\t        let post = AuthState {\n1506\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1507\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1508\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1509\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1510\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1511\t            revoked_sessions:
