{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_fd7f0606cf544c6cb6bcd11c","type":"tool_result","content":"0\t# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n1\t\n2\t**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n3\t\n4\tMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n5\t\n6\t---\n7\t\n8\t## Table of Contents\n9\t\n10\t1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n11\t2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n12\t3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n13\t4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n14\t5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n15\t6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n16\t7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n17\t8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n18\t9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n19\t10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n20\t11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n21\t12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n22\t13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n23\t14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n24\t15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n25\t\n26\t---\n27\t\n28\t## 1. Design Principles\n29\t\n30\t1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n31\t2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n32\t3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n33\t4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n34\t5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n35\t6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n36\t7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n37\t8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n38\t\n39\t### Key constraints\n40\t\n41\t- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n42\t- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n43\t- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n44\t- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n45\t\n46\t---\n47\t\n48\t## 2. Architecture\n49\t\n50\t### Core model\n51\t\n52\tMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n53\t\n54\t- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n55\t- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n56\t- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n57\t- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n58\t- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n59\t- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n60\t- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n61\t- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n62\t- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n63\t\n64\t```\n65\tClient\n66\t  │\n67\t  ▼\n68\tMiroir Orchestrator\n69\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n70\t  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n71\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n72\t\n73\t  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n74\t  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n75\t  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n76\t  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n77\t  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n78\t  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Replication factor\n82\t\n83\t| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n84\t|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n85\t| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n86\t| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n87\t| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n88\t\n89\t### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n90\t\n91\tMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n92\t\n93\t```\n94\tnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\n95\tassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n96\t                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n97\t\n98\twrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n99\t                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n100\t\n101\tsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\n102\tsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\tWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n106\t\n107\tProperties:\n108\t- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n109\t- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n110\t- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n111\t- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n112\t- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n113\t\n114\tThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n115\t\n116\t### Write path\n117\t\n118\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n119\t2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n120\t3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n121\t4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n122\t5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n123\t6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n124\t7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n125\t8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n126\t\n127\t**Write quorum — two rules.**\n128\t\n129\t1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n130\t2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n131\t\n132\tExamples:\n133\t\n134\t| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n135\t|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n136\t| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n137\t| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n138\t| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n139\t| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n140\t| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n141\t\n142\tDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n143\t\n144\t**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n145\t\n146\t**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n147\t\n148\t### Read path\n149\t\n150\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n151\t2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n152\t3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n153\t4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n154\t5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n155\t6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n156\t7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n157\t8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n158\t9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n159\t\n160\tThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n161\t\n162\t**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n163\t\n164\t**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n165\t\n166\t**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n167\t\n168\t### Replica groups in practice\n169\t\n170\tReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n171\t\n172\t```\n173\tExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n174\t\n175\tGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n176\t  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n177\t  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n178\t  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n179\t  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n180\t  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n181\t  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n182\t\n183\tWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\n184\tQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\n185\tQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\n186\tQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n187\t```\n188\t\n189\tWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n190\t\n191\t**Tradeoffs table**\n192\t\n193\t| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n194\t|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n195\t| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n196\t| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n197\t| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n198\t| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n199\t| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_3c3143cab492475487a4ee6a","session_id":"ea878048-2e42-4b12-a662-8906952566a7","uuid":"fbb2b00c-44d4-4118-a5ab-70b9cd2afe47","timestamp":"2026-05-22T19:29:45.895Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_50f5209653754752a8c7f7d3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Keys management endpoints: CRUD with broadcast to all nodes.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements P2.4:\n4\t//! - `POST /keys` — create key on every node (all-or-nothing)\n5\t//! - `PATCH /keys/{key}` — update key on every node (sequential with rollback)\n6\t//! - `DELETE /keys/{key}` — delete key on every node (all-or-nothing)\n7\t//! - `GET /keys` — list keys (proxy to first node)\n8\t//! - `GET /keys/{key}` — get key (proxy to first node)\n9\t\n10\tuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\n11\tuse axum::http::StatusCode;\n12\tuse axum::routing::{get, post};\n13\tuse axum::{Json, Router};\n14\tuse miroir_core::api_error::{MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\n15\tuse miroir_core::config::Config;\n16\tuse serde_json::Value;\n17\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n18\t\n19\tuse crate::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\n20\t\n21\t/// Collect all node addresses from config.\n22\tfn all_node_addresses(config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {\n23\t    config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect()\n24\t}\n25\t\n26\t/// Try to forward a Meilisearch error; fall back to a Miroir error.\n27\tfn forward_or_miroir(_status: u16, body: &str, fallback_msg: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n28\t    if let Some(meili_err) = MeilisearchError::forwarded(body) {\n29\t        meili_err\n30\t    } else {\n31\t        MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::NoQuorum, fallback_msg)\n32\t    }\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n36\twhere\n37\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n38\t{\n39\t    Router::new()\n40\t        .route(\"/\", post(create_key_handler).get(list_keys_handler))\n41\t        .route(\n42\t            \"/:key\",\n43\t            get(get_key_handler).patch(update_key_handler).delete(delete_key_handler),\n44\t        )\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t// POST /keys — create key (all-or-nothing broadcast)\n49\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n50\t\n51\tasync fn create_key_handler(\n52\t    Extension(config): Extension<Arc<Config>>,\n53\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n54\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, MeilisearchError> {\n55\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n56\t    let nodes = all_node_addresses(&config);\n57\t    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n58\t    let mut first_response: Option<Value> = None;\n59\t\n60\t    for address in &nodes {\n61\t        match client.post_raw(address, \"/keys\", &body).await {\n62\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n63\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n64\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n65\t                }\n66\t                created_on.push(address.clone());\n67\t            }\n68\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n69\t                // Rollback: delete key on all previously created nodes\n70\t                rollback_delete_key(&client, &body, &created_on).await;\n71\t                let msg = format!(\n72\t                    \"key creation failed on {}: HTTP {} — {}\",\n73\t                    address, status, text\n74\t                );\n75\t                return Err(forward_or_miroir(status, &text, &msg));\n76\t            }\n77\t            Err(e) => {\n78\t                rollback_delete_key(&client, &body, &created_on).await;\n79\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n80\t                    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n81\t                    format!(\"key creation failed on {}: {}\", address, e),\n82\t                ));\n83\t            }\n84\t        }\n85\t    }\n86\t\n87\t    Ok(Json(first_response.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({\"status\": \"created\"}))))\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\t/// Rollback by deleting the key from nodes where it was successfully created.\n91\tasync fn rollback_delete_key(\n92\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n93\t    body: &Value,\n94\t    nodes: &[String],\n95\t) {\n96\t    // Try to get the key UID from the creation body or extract it\n97\t    let key_or_name = body\n98\t        .get(\"uid\")\n99\t        .or(body.get(\"name\"))\n100\t        .or(body.get(\"key\"))\n101\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n102\t        .unwrap_or(\"\");\n103\t\n104\t    if key_or_name.is_empty() {\n105\t        tracing::warn!(\"key rollback: cannot determine key identifier for rollback\");\n106\t        return;\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    for address in nodes {\n110\t        let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", key_or_name);\n111\t        match client.delete_raw(address, &path).await {\n112\t            Ok(_) => tracing::info!(node = %address, \"key rollback: deleted key\"),\n113\t            Err(e) => tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"key rollback: failed to delete key\"),\n114\t        }\n115\t    }\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n119\t// PATCH /keys/{key} — update key (sequential broadcast with rollback)\n120\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n121\t\n122\tasync fn update_key_handler(\n123\t    Path(key): Path<String>,\n124\t    Extension(config): rollback_key_update(\n190\t    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n191\t    path: &str,\n192\t    snapshots: &[(String, Value)],\n193\t    applied: &[String],\n194\t) {\n195\t    for address in applied tracing::error!(node = %address, error = %e, \"key rollback failed\");\n206\t                }\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t    }\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// \"deleted\"}))))\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n264\t// GET /keys — list keys (proxy to first node)\n265\t// Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n1013-| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n1014-\n1015:### Bearer token dispatch\n1016-\n1017-Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n1018-\n1019:[Omitted long matching line]\n1020-\n1021-[Omitted long context line]\n1022:2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n1023-   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n1024-   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n1025:   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n1026-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n1027-3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n1028:4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n1029:[Omitted long matching line]\n1030-   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n1031-   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n1032-   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n--\n2903-\n2904-**Problem.** Every settings change, ranking-rule tweak, Meilisearch upgrade, or Miroir config change carries risk. Validating against real production traffic is the only reliable way — but production is the scariest place to experiment. Teams want a production-fidelity staging signal without production-scale risk.\n2905-\n2906:**Mechanism.** Configure one or more shadow targets (another Miroir or a standalone Meilisearch). For a configurable fraction of incoming requests, Miroir asynchronously dispatches the same request to the shadow **after** returning the primary's response to the client:\n2907-\n2908-```\n2909-client ──→ Miroir ──→ primary cluster ──→ response to client (synchronous)\n--\n3438-\n3439-| Path | Cost | Scaling |\n3440-|------|------|---------|\n3441:| Scatter fan-out dispatch | ~5% of 1 core per 1 kQPS | Linear in RG × RF |\n3442-| JSON parse + re-serialize | ~30% of 1 core per 1 kQPS of 10 KB responses | Streaming bodies where possible |\n3443-| Global result merge | ~5% (min-heap; O(n log k)) | Dominated by hit count |\n3444-| Rendezvous hash per doc | <1% | Tiny xxhash cost |"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_3c3143cab492475487a4ee6a","session_id":"ea878048-2e42-4b12-a662-8906952566a7","uuid":"4540d84a-9322-4c52-8ec7-ac0fc2e86ab2","timestamp":"2026-05-22T19:29:51.003Z"}

RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner canary_latency_ms\");\n651\t            let a = CounterVec::new(\n652\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_canary_assertion_failures_total\", \"Canary assertion failures\"),\n653\t                &[\"canary\", \"assertion_type\"],\n654\t            ).expect(\"create canary_assertion_failures_total\");\n655\t            reg!(r); reg!(l); reg!(a);\n656\t            (Some(r), Some(l), Some(a))\n657\t        } else {\n658\t            (None, None, None)\n659\t        };\n660\t\n661\t        // ── §13.19 Admin UI metrics ──\n662\t        let (\n663\t ).expect(\"create explain_execute_total\");\n700\t            reg!(r); reg!(w); reg!(e);\n701\t            (Some(r), Some(w), Some(e))\n702\t        } else {\n703\t            (None, None,       reg!(memory_pressure);\n763\t        reg!(cpu_throttled_seconds_total);\n764\t        reg!(request_queue_depth);\n765\t        reg!(background_queue_depth);\n766\t Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n880\t        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n881\t        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n882\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n883\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n884\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n885\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n886\t        })?)\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n890\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n891\t    }\n892\t\n893\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n894\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n895\t    }\n896\t}\n897\t\n898\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n899\t///\n900\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n901\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n902\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n903\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n904\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n905\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n906\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n907\t\n908\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n909\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n910\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n911\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n912\t\n913\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n914\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n915\t}\n916\t\n917\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n918\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n919\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n920\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n921\t}\n922\t\n923\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n924\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n925\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n926\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n927\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n928\t    }\n929\t\n930\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n931\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n932\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n933\t        }\n934\t    }\n935\t}\n936\t\n937\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n938\t///\n939\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n940\t/// is accurately decremented.\n941\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n942\t    metrics: Metrics,\n943\t    request_id: String,\n944\t}\n945\t\n946\timpl InFlightGuard {\n947\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n948\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n949\t        tracing::trace!(\n950\t            request_id = %request_id,\n951\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n952\t            \"request started\"\n953\t        );\n954\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n955\t    }\n956\t}\n957\t\n958\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n959\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n960\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n961\t        tracing::trace!(\n962\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n963\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n964\t            \"request completed\"\n965\t        );\n966\t    }\n967\t}\n968\t\n969\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n970\t///\n971\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n972\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n973\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n974\t    request\n975\t        .extensions()\n976\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n977\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n978\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n979\t        .to_string()\n980\t}\n981\t\n982\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n983\t///\n984\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n985\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n986\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n987\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n988\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n989\t    mut req: Request,\n990\t    next: Next,\n991\t) -> Response {\n992\t    let start = Instant::now();\n993\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n994\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n995\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n996\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n997\t\n998\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n999\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1000\t    let request_id = req\n1001\t        .headers()\n1002\t        .get_request_id()\n1003\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1004\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1005\t\n1006\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1007\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1008\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1009\t    let span = info_span!(\n1010\t        \"request\",\n1011\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1012\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1013\t        method = %method,\n1014\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1015\t    );\n1016\t\n1017\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1018\t\n1019\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1020\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1021\t\n1022\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1023\t\n1024\t    drop(in_flight);\n1025\t\n1026\t    let status = response.status();\n1027\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1028\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1029\t\n1030\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1031\t    metrics\n1032\t        .request_duration\n1033\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1034\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1035\t    metrics\n1036\t        .requests_total\n1037\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1038\t        .inc();\n1039\t\n1040\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1041\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1042\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1043\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1044\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1045\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1046\t        tracing::error!(\n1047\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1048\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1049\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1050\t            message = %message,\n1051\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1052\t            status = status_u16,\n1053\t            method = %method,\n1054\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1055\t        );\n1056\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1057\t        tracing::warn!(\n1058\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1059\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1060\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1061\t            message = %message,\n1062\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1063\t            status = status_u16,\n1064\t            method = %method,\n1065\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1066\t        );\n1067\t    } else {\n1068\t        tracing::info!(\n1069\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1070\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1071\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1072\t            message = %message,\n1073\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1074\t            status = status_u16,\n1075\t            method = %method,\n1076\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1077\t        );\n1078\t    }\n1079\t\n1080\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1081\t    let mut response = response;\n1082\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1083\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1084\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1085\t        }\n1086\t    }\n1087\t\n1088\t    response\n1089\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1092\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1093\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1094\t}\n1095\t\n1096\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1097\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n1098\t    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1099\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1100\t        Err(e) => {\n1101\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1102\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1103\t        }\n1104\t    }\n1105\t}\n1106\t\n1107\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1108\timpl Metrics {\n1109\t    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n1110\t\n1111\t    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n1112\t [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n20-10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n--\n30-\n31-1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n32-2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n33-3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n34-4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n35:5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n36-6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n37-7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n38-8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n39-\n40-### Key constraints\n--\n118-\n119-1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n120-2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n121-3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n122-4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n123:5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n124-6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n125-7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n126-8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n127-\n128-**Write quorum — two rules.**\n--\n150-\n151-1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n152-2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n153-3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n154-4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n155:5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n156-6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n157-7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n158-8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n159-9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n160-\n--\n219-**Adding a node to an existing group (capacity scaling; movement: ~1/(Ng+1) of that group's documents):**\n220-1. Assign the new node to a group (config `replica_group` field); mark `joining`\n221-2. Recompute shard assignments within that group — approximately `S/(Ng+1)` shards now prefer the new node\n222-3. Dual-write: new inbound writes for affected shards in this group go to both the old owner and the new node\n223-4. Background migration: for each affected shard, use `GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={id}&limit=1000&offset=...` to page through only that shard's documents on the source node; write each page to the new node\n224:5. Once all pages are transferred, mark node `active`; stop dual-write; delete migrated shard from old node with `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`\n225-\n226-Documents on unaffected shards, and all documents in other groups, are never touched.\n227-\n228-**Adding a new replica group (throughput scaling; no migration of existing groups):**\n229-1. Provision new nodes and assign them to a new group ID in config\n230-2. Mark the new group `initializing` — queries are not yet routed to it\n231-3. Background sync: for each shard, copy all documents from any healthy existing group to the new group's nodes using `filter=_miroir_shard={id}` pagination; new inbound writes fan out to the new group immediately\n232-4. Once all shards are fully synced, mark the group `active` — queries begin routing to it in round-robin\n233:5. Existing groups continue serving queries throughout; there is no read interruption\n234-\n235-**Removing a node from a group (movement: ~RF/Ng of that group's documents):**\n236-1. Mark node `draining`; stop routing writes for its shards to it\n237-2. Recompute assignments within the group — affected shards reassigned to surviving nodes\n238-3. Background migration: copy affected shards to new owners using `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`\n--\n325-Per document:\n326-1. Extract primary key value\n327-2. `shard_id = hash(primary_key) % S`\n328-3. Nodes = rendezvous top-RF for `shard_id`\n329-4. Inject `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` into the document\n330:5. Assign document to those nodes\n331-\n332-Per batch — group documents by target node set, one batched request per node:\n333-```\n334-Original: [doc-A (shard 3), doc-B (shard 7), doc-C (shard 3), doc-D (shard 11)]\n335-\n--\n912-   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=0\n913-   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=1000\n914-   ... (until exhausted)\n915-   ```\n916-4. Write each page to the new node (documents already carry `_miroir_shard`)\n917:5. Once all pages transferred, mark node `active` for those shards\n918-6. Stop dual-write; delete the migrated shard from the old node:\n919-   ```\n920-   POST /indexes/{uid}/documents/delete  {\"filter\": \"_miroir_shard = {shard_id}\"}\n921-   ```\n922-7. Documents on unaffected shards are never read, written, or touched\n923-\n924-**Performance**: with S=64 shards, N=3 nodes, adding node-3 migrates `~16` shards. Each migration reads only that shard's documents (roughly `total_docs / 64` documents per shard), not the full node corpus. Total data moved is approximately `total_docs / 4` — the theoretical optimum for a 4-node cluster.\n925-\n926----\n927-\n928:## 5. API Surface and Compatibility\n929-\n930-Miroir exposes the Meilisearch REST API verbatim. All request and response shapes, status codes, error formats, and headers must match the Meilisearch spec exactly.\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 100]"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_3c3143cab492475487a4ee6a","session_id":"ea878048-2e42-4b12-a662-8906952566a7","uuid":"8e0acd28-a9f4-4fe2-b74a-9b07b7e8290a","timestamp":"2026-05-22T19:29:59.707Z"}

Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n4\t//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n5\t//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n8\t//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n9\t//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n10\t//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n11\t\n12\tuse axum::{\n13\t    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n14\t    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n15\t    middleware::Next,\n16\t    response::{IntoResponse,
