-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT signing and validation — primary secret\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn sign_and_validate_primary_jwt() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let claims = state.validate_jwt(&token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claims.sub, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.idx, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.scope, \"search\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn signed_jwt_authenticates_via_dispatch() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn expired_jwt_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now - 3600,\n            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        // Tamper with the signature\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n\n        // Sign token with the previous secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Old token should still validate via previous secret\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&old_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user1\");\n\n        // And dispatch should authenticate it\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&old_token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_new_token_validates_via_primary_secret() {\n        let state = test_state_with_dual_jwt();\n        let new_token = state.sign_jwt(\"user2\", \"orders\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let validated = state.validate_jwt(&new_token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(validated.sub, \"user2\");\n        assert_eq!(validated.idx, \"orders\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_wrong_secret_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"correct-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(\"previous-secret-key-32bytes-long!!\".to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Token signed with a completely different secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            \"wrong-secret-key-32bytes-long!!!!\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn leak_response_empty_previous_rejects_old_tokens() {\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"new-primary-secret-key-32bytes!!\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(String::new()), // empty = leak response\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJ0ZXN0In0.fake\");\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_after_step5_steady_state_previous_removed() {\n        let primary = \"final-primary-secret-key-32bytes\";\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n\n        // Tokens signed with current primary work\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&token).is_ok());\n\n        // Old tokens signed with now-removed previous fail\n        let old_claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: epoch_seconds() - 100,\n            exp: epoch_seconds() + 800,\n        };\n        let old_header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(\n            &old_header,\n            &old_claims,\n            \"old-previous-secret-now-removed\".as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n        assert!(state.validate_jwt(&old_token).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // End-to-end rotation scenario\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n\n        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n        let pre = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n\n        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n        let during = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // Old token still validates\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n        // New tokens work too\n        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n\n        // Post-rotation: only v2\n        let post = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        };\n        // New token still works\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n        // Old token is rejected (signed with v1, no previous loaded)\n        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_err());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match (admin_key only)\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_matches_admin_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"admin-key-456\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/some/endpoint\",\n            Some(\"master-key-123\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n     Some(\"wrong-key\"),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_rejects_missing_auth() {\n        let state = assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths only)\n    // — missing auth → 401 miroir_invalid_auth\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn missing_auth_on_gated_endpoint_returns_invalid_auth() assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_matches_admin_key() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"X-Admin-Key\", \"admin-key-456\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert!(check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_rejects_wrong_key() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"X-Admin-Key\", \"wrong-key\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert!(!check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn x_admin_key_missing_header() {\n        let headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        assert!(!check_x_admin_key(&headers, b\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Constant-time comparison\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_matching() {\n        assert!(constant_time_compare(b\"hello\", b\"hello\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_not_matching() {\n        assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"hello\", b\"world\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_different_lengths() {\n        assert!(!constant_time_compare(b\"short\", b\"much-longer-value\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_eq_empty() {\n        assert!(constant_time_compare(b\"\", b\"\"));\n    }\n\n    /// Timing-injection harness: verify no measurable delta between\n    /// \"all bytes wrong\" and \"one byte wrong\" comparisons at the same length.\n    #[test]\n    fn constant_time_no_timing_leak() {\n        use std::time::Instant;\n\n        let expected = b\"admin-key-456\";\n        let all_wrong = b\"xxxxxxxxxxxxx\";\n        let one_wrong = b\"admin-key-457\";\n\n        let iterations = 100_000u64;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(all_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let all_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(one_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let one_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let ratio = all_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64() / one_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64();\n        assert!(\n            ratio > 0.5 && ratio < 2.0,\n            \"Timing ratio {} suggests non-constant-time comparison: all_wrong={:?}, one_wrong={:?}\",\n            ratio,\n            all_wrong_duration,\n            one_wrong_duration,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Bearer extraction\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_valid() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"authorization\", \"Bearer my-token\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), Some(\"my-token\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_missing_header() {\n        let headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn extract_bearer_wrong_scheme() {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(\"authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\".parse().unwrap());\n        assert_eq!(extract_bearer(&headers), None);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Admin path detection\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn admin_path_detected() {\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/metrics\"));\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/admin/login\"));\n        assert!(is_admin_path(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/en\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn non_admin_path_not_detected() {\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/indexes/products\"));\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/search/products\"));\n        assert!(!is_admin_path(\"/health\"));\n    }\n\n    // rate_limiter_always_allows() {\n        let limiter = RateLimiter;\n        assert!(limiter.check(&RateLimitBucket::AdminLogin(\"127.0.0.1\".into())).is_ok());\n        assert!(limiter.check(&RateLimitBucket::SearchUi(\"10.0.0.1\".into())).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // AuthVerdict helpers\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_131e3bd2c3e542649c6760a7","type":"tool_result","content":"1000\t\n1001\t| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n1002\t|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n1003\t| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n1004\t| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n1005\t| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n1006\t| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n1007\t| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n1008\t| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n1009\t| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n1010\t| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n1011\t| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n1012\t| `X-CSRF-Token` | Request | Admin UI CSRF double-submit token; required on state-changing requests from the Admin UI session cookie path. Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n1013\t| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n1014\t\n1015\t### Bearer token dispatch\n1016\t\n1017\tThree token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n1018\t\n1019\t0. **Dispatch-exempt check (pre-empts all other rules).** If the request's (method, path) is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list in rule 5, the endpoint's handler runs **directly** — rules 1–4 do NOT execute, regardless of whether `Authorization` parses as a JWT, matches an opaque key, or is absent entirely. A valid search UI JWT sent to, say, `GET /_miroir/metrics` is not subject to the rule-1 scope check because `/_miroir/metrics` is dispatch-exempt and its handler decides auth on its own terms.\n1020\t\n1021\t1. **JWT-shape probe.** Miroir first attempts to parse the bearer token as a JWT (three dot-separated base64url segments with a `kid`/`alg` header). If parsing succeeds, the token is validated as a search UI JWT (signature against the active JWT secret(s) per §9 \"JWT signing-secret rotation\", exp/nbf, `kid`, `idx` claim, `scope` claim). A parseable-but-invalid signature/exp/kid returns HTTP 401 `miroir_jwt_invalid` — it does **not** fall through to the opaque-token paths below. Signature-valid tokens then pass through the orchestrator-level scope check: the request's (method + path) must correspond to an action in `scope` and the target index must equal `idx`, otherwise HTTP 403 `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` is returned before any node is called. See §13.21.\n1022\t2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n1023\t   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n1024\t   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n1025\t   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n1026\t   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n1027\t3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n1028\t4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n1029\t5. **Dispatch-exempt endpoints.** Endpoints marked as **dispatch-exempt** skip the auth dispatch chain above entirely; their handlers implement their own auth logic (body-based for admin login, mode-based for the search UI session endpoint, rate-limited for public paths, optional for metrics). \"Dispatch-exempt\" here means \"the bearer-token dispatch does not attempt to match `admin_key` / `master_key` against the `Authorization` header for this method+path; the endpoint's own handler performs whatever auth is required.\" The full exhaustive list:\n1030\t   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n1031\t   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n1032\t   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n1033\t   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode` (`public` / `shared_key` via `X-Search-UI-Key` header / `oauth_proxy` via upstream-proxy headers); see §13.21.\n1034\t   - `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point (static assets); IP rate-limited only.\n1035\t\n1036\t`X-Admin-Key` (admin endpoints) and `X-Search-UI-Key` (session-minting endpoint) are evaluated independently of the bearer chain above — they short-circuit directly into their respective code paths.\n1037\t\n1038\t---\n1039\t\n1040\t## 6. Deployment\n1041\t\n1042\t### Helm chart structure\n1043\t\n1044\t```\n1045\tcharts/miroir/\n1046\t├── Chart.yaml\n1047\t├── values.yaml\n1048\t├── values.schema.json\n1049\t├── templates/\n1050\t│   ├── _helpers.tpl\n1051\t│   ├── miroir-deployment.yaml\n1052\t│   ├── miroir-service.yaml\n1053\t│   ├── miroir-headless.yaml\n1054\t│   ├── miroir-configmap.yaml\n1055\t│   ├── miroir-secret.yaml\n1056\t│   ├── miroir-hpa.yaml\n1057\t│   ├── miroir-pvc.yaml         (optional; rendered only when cdc.buffer.primary=pvc or cdc.buffer.overflow=pvc — §13.13)\n1058\t│   ├── meilisearch-statefulset.yaml\n1059\t│   ├── meilisearch-service.yaml\n1060\t│   ├── redis-deployment.yaml   (when taskStore.backend=redis)\n1061\t│   ├── serviceaccount.yaml\n1062\t│   └── NOTES.txt\n1063\t└── tests/\n1064\t    └── connection-test.yaml\n1065\t```\n1066\t\n1067\t### Key values\n1068\t\n1069\t> **Dev vs. production defaults.** The Helm chart ships \"dev-sized\" defaults: a single Miroir pod, SQLite task store, `replicationFactor: 1`, no Redis, and HPA disabled. These defaults boot a working single-pod install for evaluation and CI. **For production**, override to `miroir.replicas: 2` (minimum), `miroir.replicationFactor: 2`, `redis.enabled: true`, `taskStore.backend: redis`, and `miroir.hpa.enabled: true`. The config-file schema in Section 4 (`replication_factor: 2`, Redis-backed task store) reflects the production story; `values.schema.json` rejects incompatible combinations (e.g., `replicas > 1` with `taskStore.backend: sqlite`). §14 sizing, the HPA in §14.4, and the §14.7 baseline row all assume the production configuration.\n1070\t\n1071\t```yaml\n1072\tmiroir:\n1073\t  image:\n1074\t    repository: ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir\n1075\t    tag: \"\"          # defaults to Chart.appVersion\n1076\t  replicas: 1        # dev default: override to 2+ in production (requires taskStore.backend=redis)\n1077\t  shards: 64\n1078\t  replicationFactor: 1   # dev default: override to 2 in production (intra-group HA replicas per shard)\n1079\t  replicaGroups: 1       # dev default: override to 2 in production (independent query pools; 1 = no throughput scaling)\n1080\t  existingSecret: \"\" # name of K8s Secret with masterKey, nodeMasterKey, adminApiKey\n1081\t  scatter:\n1082\t    unavailableShardPolicy: partial\n1083\t  hpa:\n1084\t    enabled: false       # dev default; production: true (see §14.4)\n1085\t  ingress:\n1086\t    enabled: false\n1087\t    host: search.example.com\n1088\t  serviceMonitor:\n1089\t    enabled: false\n1090\t\n1091\tmeilisearch:\n1092\t  image:\n1093\t    repository: getmeili/meilisearch\n1094\t    tag: v1.37.0\n1095\t  # Total nodes = replicaGroups × nodesPerGroup.\n1096\t  # The chart partitions nodes into groups automatically (node-0..Ng-1 → group 0, etc.).\n1097\t  replicas: 2           # dev default: 1 group × 2 nodes = 2. Production: replicaGroups × nodesPerGroup (e.g., 4 for RG=2, Ng=2)\n1098\t  nodesPerGroup: 2      # derived from replicas / replicaGroups if omitted\n1099\t  existingSecret: \"\"\n1100\t  persistence:\n1101\t    size: 50Gi\n1102\t  podAntiAffinity:\n1103\t    enabled: true\n1104\t    topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname\n1105\t\n1106\tredis:\n1107\t  enabled: false\n1108\t```\n1109\t\n1110\t### ConfigMap — node list from StatefulSet DNS\n1111\t\n1112\tThe Miroir ConfigMap is generated from Helm values. No shard migration needed.\n1169\t\n1170\t**Horizontal (add nodes):**\n1171\t1. Increment `meilisearch.replicas`\n1172\t2. ArgoCD provisions the new pod and PVC\n1173\t3. Register the new node: `POST /_miroir/nodes` (or update config)\n1174\t4. Rebalancer migrates affected shards to the new node\n1175\t5. Monitor: `GET /_miroir/rebalance/status`\n1176\t\n1177\t**Scale down (remove a node):**\n1178\t1. `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` — rebalancer migrates shards off\n1179\t2. Manually delete the released PVC\n1181\t\n1182\t### Resource sizing reference\n1183\t\n1184\tNode RAM is sized by the data each node holds: `(corpus_size / S) × shards_per_node × 1.5`. With group-scoped assignment, each node in a group holds `S / nodes_per_group` shards, each containing `corpus_size / S` data → `corpus_size / nodes_per_group × 1.5`.\n1185\t\n1186\t| Corpus | RG | RF | Nodes/group | Total nodes | Node RAM | Node storage | S |\n1187\t|--------|----|----|-------------|-------------|----------|--------------|---|\n1188\t| ≤ 10 GB | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 8 GB | 20 GB | 16 |\n1189\t| ≤ 10 GB | 2 | 1 | 2 | 4 | 8 GB | 20 GB | 16 |\n1190\t| ≤ 50 GB | 1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 32 GB | 50 GB | 32 |\n1191\t| ≤ 50 GB | 2 | 1 | 3 | 6 | 16 GB | 50 GB | 32 |\n1192\t| ≤ 200 GB | 2 | 1 | 4 | 8 | 64 GB | 100 GB | 64 |\n1193\t| ≤ 200 GB | 4 | 1 | 3 | 12 | 128 GB | 100 GB | 32 |\n1194\t| ≤ 1 TB | 2 | 1 | 6 | 12 | 256 GB | 200 GB | 64 |\n1195\t\n1196\tWhen choosing between more groups or more nodes per group: more groups means higher search throughput with the same per-node storage; more nodes per group means lower per-node storage with the same throughput.\n1197\t\n1198\t### Upgrade procedure\n1199\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ea878048-2e42-4b12-a662-8906952566a7","uuid":"2b8b32c0-934e-4991-83b4-6051d12fdbe3","timestamp":"2026-05-22T19:30:43.469Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"\n| Header | Direction | Semantics | Defined in |\n|--------|-----------|-----------|------------|\n| `X-Miroir-Degraded` | Response | Write or read completed with reduced redundancy. For writes: lists groups or shards that missed per-group quorum (§2 write path). For reads: lists shards that could not be covered. Absence of the header means full coverage. | §2 write path, §3 `unavailable_shard_policy` |\n| `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` | Response | Monotonically increasing version of the committed index settings. Stamped on responses after a successful two-phase settings commit. Clients may echo this value back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` on subsequent reads for session-consistent behavior. | §13.5 |\n| `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` | Request | Client-supplied floor (`u64`) for settings freshness. Request header; the orchestrator excludes nodes with `node_settings_version` below this floor when building a covering set (§13.5), and returns HTTP 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` only if no covering set can be assembled after exclusions. Intended to be populated from a previously observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version`. | §13.5 |\n| `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` | Response | Warning: the response was served while a two-phase settings broadcast was mid-flight (propose / verify window). Scores across shards may not be comparable until commit completes. | §13.5 |\n| `X-Miroir-Session` | Request and response | Opaque client-chosen session UUID enabling read-your-writes semantics. Miroir pins subsequent reads to the group that ACKd the session's latest write and/or blocks until its task succeeds, per `session_pinning.wait_strategy`. | §13.6 |\n| `Idempotency-Key` | Request | Client-supplied UUID de-duplicating retried writes. Body-hash-matched hits return the original `miroir_task_id`; body mismatches return HTTP 409 `miroir_idempotency_key_reused`. Also used internally for the synthesized key that makes `scatter.retry_on_timeout` safe (Section 4). | §13.10 |\n| `X-Miroir-Over-Fetch` | Request | Per-request override of `vector_search.over_fetch_factor`. Integer ≥ 1. Raises per-shard `limit` to `requested_limit × factor` so the global reranker can recover correct ordering on sparse semantic matches. Ignored for pure-keyword queries. | §13.12 |\n| `X-Miroir-Tenant` | Request | Tenant identifier used when `tenant_affinity.mode: header` to pin reads to `group = hash(tenant_id) % RG` (or the group assigned in `static_map`). Writes still fan out to all groups. Unknown tenants follow `tenant_affinity.fallback`. Ignored unless tenant affinity is enabled in `header` mode. | §13.15 |\n| `X-Admin-Key` | Request | Alternative to `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` for admin endpoints and the admin UI. Equivalent in scope and gating; useful for browser fetches where the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Rejected with HTTP 401 if the key does not match `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`. | §13.19 |\n| `X-CSRF-Token` | Request | Admin UI CSRF double-submit token; required on state-changing requests from the Admin UI session cookie path. Rejected with HTTP 403 if absent or mismatched against the token bound to the session cookie. See §9 CSRF posture. | §13.19 |\n| `X-Search-UI-Key` | Request | Shared key for `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`; required to mint a search UI JWT session via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`. Rejected with HTTP 401 if absent or mismatched. See §13.21. | §13.21 |\n\n### Bearer token dispatch\n\nThree token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically in the following order:\n\n0. **Dispatch-exempt check (pre-empts all other rules).** If the request's (method, path) is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list in rule 5, the endpoint's handler runs **directly** — rules 1–4 do NOT execute, regardless of whether `Authorization` parses as a JWT, matches an opaque key, or is absent entirely. A valid search UI JWT sent to, say, `GET /_miroir/metrics` is not subject to the rule-1 scope check because `/_miroir/metrics` is dispatch-exempt and its handler decides auth on its own terms.\n\n1. **JWT-shape probe.** Miroir first attempts to parse the bearer token as a JWT (three dot-separated base64url segments with a `kid`/`alg` header). If parsing succeeds, the token is validated as a search UI JWT (signature against the active JWT secret(s) per §9 \"JWT signing-secret rotation\", exp/nbf, `kid`, `idx` claim, `scope` claim). A parseable-but-invalid signature/exp/kid returns HTTP 401 `miroir_jwt_invalid` — it does **not** fall through to the opaque-token paths below. Signature-valid tokens then pass through the orchestrator-level scope check: the request's (method + path) must correspond to an action in `scope` and the target index must equal `idx`, otherwise HTTP 403 `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` is returned before any node is called. See §13.21.\n2. **Admin-path opaque-token match.** If parsing fails (opaque token) and the request path begins with `/_miroir/`, Miroir matches the token against `admin_key`. The following dispatch-exempt exceptions apply (see rule 5 below for the exhaustive list and semantics):\n   - `/_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional (honored when present, not required).\n   - `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated; does not consult any key.\n   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials carried in the request body as `{admin_key}`, not in `Authorization`; dispatch is bypassed and the login handler performs its own constant-time credential comparison and IP-based rate-limiting (`miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`, §4).\n   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — authentication depends on `search_ui.auth.mode`: `public` is unauthenticated and rate-limited by source IP; `shared_key` requires the `X-Search-UI-Key` header (NOT a bearer token); `oauth_proxy` requires upstream auth-proxy headers such as `X-Forwarded-User` / `X-Forwarded-Groups`. The handler performs whichever check applies to the active mode.\n3. **Master-key match.** Otherwise (opaque token on a non-`/_miroir/` path), Miroir matches against `master_key`.\n4. **Mismatch.** All three paths that require auth return HTTP 401 with `miroir_invalid_auth` on mismatch. Requests that omit `Authorization` entirely on an auth-gated endpoint also return `miroir_invalid_auth` — except on the dispatch-exempt endpoints listed in rule 5, where the handler's own auth logic determines the response.\n5. **Dispatch-exempt endpoints.** Endpoints marked as **dispatch-exempt** skip the auth dispatch chain above entirely; their handlers implement their own auth logic (body-based for admin login, mode-based for the search UI session endpoint, rate-limited for public paths, optional for metrics). \"Dispatch-exempt\" here means \"the bearer-token dispatch does not attempt to match `admin_key` / `master_key` against the `Authorization` header for this method+path; the endpoint's own handler performs whatever auth is required.\" The full exhaustive list:\n   - `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional; handler accepts the request with or without `Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>`.\n   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch for the end-user SPA.\n   - `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body (`{admin_key}`); handler does constant-time comparison plus `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` rate-limiting and exponential backoff (§4).\n   - `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode` (`public` / `shared_key` via `X-Search-UI-Key` header / `oauth_proxy` via upstream-proxy headers); see §13.21.\n   - `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point (static assets); IP rate-limited only.\n\n`X-Admin-Key` (admin endpoints) and `X-Search-UI-Key` (session-minting endpoint) are evaluated independently of the bearer chain above — they short-circuit directly into their respective code paths.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Deployment\n\n### Helm chart structure\n\n```\ncharts/miroir/\n├── Chart.yaml\n├── values.yaml\n├── values.schema.json\n├── templates/\n│   ├── _helpers.tpl\n│   ├── miroir-deployment.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-service.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-headless.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-configmap.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-secret.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-hpa.yaml\n│   ├── miroir-pvc.yaml         (optional; rendered only when cdc.buffer.primary=pvc or cdc.buffer.overflow=pvc — §13.13)\n│   ├── meilisearch-statefulset.yaml\n│   ├── meilisearch-service.yaml\n│   ├── redis-deployment.yaml   (when taskStore.backend=redis)\n│   ├── serviceaccount.yaml\n│   └── NOTES.txt\n└── tests/\n    └── connection-test.yaml\n```\n\n### Key values\n\n> **Dev vs. production defaults.** The Helm chart ships \"dev-sized\" defaults: a single Miroir pod, SQLite task store, `replicationFactor: 1`, no Redis, and HPA disabled. These defaults boot a working single-pod install for evaluation and CI. **For production**, override to `miroir.replicas: 2` (minimum), `miroir.replicationFactor: 2`, `redis.enabled: true`, `taskStore.backend: redis`, and `miroir.hpa.enabled: true`. The config-file schema in Section 4 (`replication_factor: 2`, Redis-backed task store) reflects the production story; `values.schema.json` rejects incompatible combinations (e.g., `replicas > 1` with `taskStore.backend: sqlite`). §14 sizing, the HPA in §14.4, and the §14.7 baseline row all assume the production configuration.\n\n```yaml\nmiroir:\n  image:\n    repository: ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir\n    tag: \"\"          # defaults to Chart.appVersion\n  replicas: 1        # dev default: override to 2+ in production (requires taskStore.backend=redis)\n  shards: 64\n  replicationFactor: 1   # dev default: override to 2 in production (intra-group HA replicas per shard)\n  replicaGroups: 1       # dev default: override to 2 in production (independent query pools; 1 = no throughput scaling)\n  existingSecret: \"\" # name of K8s Secret with masterKey, nodeMasterKey, adminApiKey\n  scatter:\n    unavailableShardPolicy: partial\n  hpa:\n    enabled: false       # dev default; production: true (see §14.4)\n  ingress:\n    enabled: false\n    host: search.example.com\n  serviceMonitor:\n    enabled: false\n\nmeilisearch:\n  image:\n    repository: getmeili/meilisearch\n    tag: v1.37.0\n  # Total nodes = replicaGroups × nodesPerGroup.\n  # The chart partitions nodes into groups automatically (node-0..Ng-1 → group 0, etc.).\n  replicas: 2           # dev default: 1 group × 2 nodes = 2. Production: replicaGroups × nodesPerGroup (e.g., 4 for RG=2, Ng=2)\n  nodesPerGroup: 2      # derived from replicas / replicaGroups if omitted\n  existingSecret: \"\"\n  persistence:\n    size: 50Gi\n  podAntiAffinity:\n    enabled: true\n    topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname\n\nredis:\n  enabled: false\n```\n\n### ConfigMap — node list from StatefulSet DNS\n\nThe Miroir ConfigMap is generated from Helm values. Node addresses use StatefulSet stable DNS:\n\n```\nhttp://<release>-meili-<n>.<release>-meili-headless.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local:7700\n```\n\nNo manual node list management required; scaling `meilisearch.replicas` automatically generates the correct node addresses.\n\n### Service topology\n\n*Production topology shown. Dev default: 1 Miroir pod, 2 Meilisearch pods, HPA disabled (see Helm values above).*\n\n```\nExternal clients → Service: miroir (ClusterIP / Ingress, :7700)\n                     ↓\n               Deployment: miroir (replicas: 2+ via HPA, see §14.4)\n               ┌──────────┬──────────┬──────────┐\n               ▼          ▼          ▼\n         meili-0      meili-1      meili-2    StatefulSet (headless)\n```\n\nAdditional services:\n- `miroir-metrics` (ClusterIP, :9090) — Prometheus scrape target\n- `miroir-headless` (Headless) — stable DNS for Miroir orchestrator pod discovery (§14.5)\n- `<name>-meili-headless` (Headless) — stable DNS for StatefulSet pods\n\n### ArgoCD Application\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Application\nmetadata:\n  name: miroir-<instance>\n  namespace: argocd\nspec:\n  project: default\n  source:\n    repoURL: https://github.com/jedarden/declarative-config\n    targetRevision: HEAD\n    path: k8s/<cluster>/miroir/<instance>\n    helm:\n      valueFiles: [values.yaml]\n  destination:\n    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc\n    namespace: <namespace>\n  syncPolicy:\n    automated:\n      prune: true\n      selfHeal: true\n    syncOptions:\n    - CreateNamespace=true\n    - ServerSideApply=true\n```\n\n### Scaling\n\n**Vertical (increase node RAM):** Update `meilisearch.resources.limits.memory` and PVC size; ArgoCD syncs; StatefulSet rolling update. No shard migration needed.\n\n**Horizontal (add nodes):**\n1. Increment `meilisearch.replicas`\n2. ArgoCD provisions the new pod and PVC\n3. Register the new node: `POST /_miroir/nodes` (or update config)\n4. Rebalancer migrates affected shards to the new node\n5. Monitor: `GET /_miroir/rebalance/status`\n\n**Scale down (remove a node):**\n1. `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` — rebalancer migrates shards off\n2. Once drain is complete, decrement `meilisearch.replicas`\n3. Manually delete the released PVC\n\n### Resource sizing reference\n\nNode RAM is sized by the data each node holds: `(corpus_size / S) × shards_per_node × 1.5`. With group-scoped assignment, each node in a group holds `S / nodes_per_group` shards, each containing `corpus_size / S` data → `corpus_size / nodes_per_group × 1.5`.\n\n| Corpus | RG | RF | Nodes/group | Total nodes | Node RAM | Node storage | S |\n|--------|----|----|-------------|-------------|----------|--------------|---|\n| ≤ 10 GB | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 8 GB | 20 GB | 16 |\n| ≤ 10 GB | 2 | 1 | 2 | 4 | 8 GB | 20 GB | 16 |\n| ≤ 50 GB | 1 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 32 GB | 50 GB | 32 |\n| ≤ 50 GB | 2 | 1 | 3 | 6 | 16 GB | 50 GB | 32 |\n| ≤ 200 GB | 2 | 1 | 4 | 8 | 64 GB | 100 GB | 64 |\n| ≤ 200 GB | 4 | 1 | 3 | 12 | 128 GB | 100 GB | 32 |\n| ≤ 1 TB | 2 | 1 | 6 | 12 | 256 GB | 200 GB | 64 |\n\nWhen choosing between more groups or more nodes per group: more groups means higher search throughput with the same per-node storage; more nodes per group means lower per-node storage with the same throughput.\n\n### Upgrade procedure\n","numLines":200,"startLine":1000,"totalLines":3756}}}

AuthState initialization and middleware wiring\n111\t\n112\t## JWT Rotation Support\n113\t\n114\tThe implementation supports seamless JWT secret rotation:\n115\t\n116\t1. **Pre-rotation**: Only primary secret active\n117\t2. **During rotation**: New primary + old primary as previous\n118\t3. **Post-rotation**: Only new primary (previous removed)\n119\t\n120\tTokens signed with either secret are accepted during the rotation window. Old tokens continue to work until they expire naturally.\n121\t\n122\t## Middleware Stack\n123\t\n124\tThe `auth_middleware` is correctly positioned in the middleware stack:\n125\t\n126\t1. `csrf_middleware` - runs first\n127\t2. `auth_middleware` - bearer-token dispatch\n128\t3. The implementation supports three token types simultaneously: `master_key`, `admin_key`, and search UI JWTs.\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Rule 0 - Dispatch-Exempt Check (`is_dispatch_exempt`)\n\nEndpoints that bypass all auth checks (handler decides auth):\n- `GET /health` - unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n- `GET /version` - unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n- `GET /_miroir/ready` - unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n- `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` - unauthenticated public locale fetch\n- `POST /_miroir/admin/login` - credentials in body\n- `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` - auth per `search_ui.auth.mode`\n- `GET /ui/search/{index}` - public SPA entry point\n\n**Note:** `GET /_miroir/metrics` requires admin_key (NOT exempt)\n\n### Rule 1 - JWT-Shape Probe\n\n- `probe_jwt_shape()`: Checks if token has JWT structure (three dot-separated base64url segments)\n- `validate_jwt()`: Full JWT validation with:\n  - HMAC-SHA256 signature verification\n  - Expiration checking with 30s leeway\n  - Support for dual secrets during rotation (primary + previous)\n  - Key ID (`kid`) header to identify which secret signed the token\n\n### Rule 2 - Admin-Path Opaque-Token Match\n\n- `is_admin_path()`: Checks if path starts with `/_miroir/`\n- For admin paths: only `admin_key` is accepted via Bearer token\n- Master key is explicitly rejected on admin paths\n\n### Rule 3 - Master-Key Match\n\n- For non-admin paths: only `master_key` is accepted via Bearer token\n- Admin key is explicitly rejected on non-admin paths\n\n### Rule 4 - Mismatch\n\n- Missing Authorization header on auth-gated endpoints → 401 `miroir_invalid_auth`\n- Invalid token → 401 `miroir_invalid_auth`\n- JWT-shaped token that fails validation → 401 `miroir_jwt_invalid`\n\n### X-Admin-Key Short-Circuit\n\n- `check_x_admin_key()`: Independent header check for admin endpoints\n- Provides alternative auth mechanism for admin operations\n- Uses constant-time comparison\n\n## Security Features\n\n### Constant-Time Comparison\n\n- All opaque-token comparisons use `subtle::ConstantTimeEq`\n- Prevents timing side-channel attacks on secret key values\n- Test `constant_time_no_timing_leak` verifies no measurable delta between \"all bytes wrong\" and \"one byte wrong\"\n\n### Rate-Limit Hooks\n\n- `RateLimitBucket` enum defines bucket key types:\n  - `AdminLogin(String)` - `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n  - `SearchUi(String)` - `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n- Phase 2 uses in-memory stub (always allows)\n- Phase 6 will back with task store (Redis/SQLite)\n\n## Test Coverage\n\nAll acceptance criteria verified with unit tests:\n\n1. ✅ Every row in plan §5 rule 5 exempt list has a unit test\n2. ✅ Opaque token on `/_miroir/*` matches only admin_key; never master_key\n3. ✅ Opaque token on other paths matches only master_key; never admin_key\n4. ✅ Missing Authorization on auth-gated endpoints → 401 `miroir_invalid_auth`\n5. ✅ `X-Admin-Key` alone gates admin endpoints equivalently to Bearer admin_key\n6. ✅ Constant-time compare: timing-injection harness shows no measurable delta\n\n**Total tests:** 62 auth tests, all passing\n\n## Configuration\n\n### AuthState Structure\n\n```rust\npub struct AuthState {\n    pub master_key: String,\n    pub admin_key: String,\n    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,        // SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET\n    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,       // SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS\n    pub seal_key: SealKey,                  // For admin session cookies\n    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n}\n```\n\n### Environment Variables\n\n- `MIROIR_MASTER_KEY` - Client-facing API key (overrides config)\n- `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY` - Admin API key (overrides config)\n- `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` - JWT signing secret (primary)\n- `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` - JWT signing secret (rotation overlap)\n- `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` - Admin session cookie sealing key\n\n## Files Modified\n\n- `crates/miroir-proxy/src/auth.rs` - Core bearer-token dispatch implementation\n- `crates/miroir-proxy/src/main.rs` - AuthState initialization and middleware wiring\n\n## JWT Rotation Support\n\nThe implementation supports seamless JWT secret rotation:\n\n1. **Pre-rotation**: Only primary secret active\n2. **During rotation**: New primary + old primary as previous\n3. **Post-rotation**: Only new primary (previous removed)\n\nTokens signed with either secret are accepted during the rotation window. Old tokens continue to work until they expire naturally.\n\n## Middleware Stack\n\nThe `auth_middleware` is correctly positioned in the middleware stack:\n\n1. `csrf_middleware` - runs first\n2. `auth_middleware` - bearer-token dispatch\n3. Extension layers\n4. `request_id_middleware` - sets X-Request-Id header\n5. `telemetry_middleware` - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span\n\n## Integration with Admin Sessions\n\nThe dispatch chain integrates with admin session cookies:\n\n1. If a sealed admin session cookie is present, it is unsealed\n2. Session ID is checked against revocation cache (Pub/Sub sync)\n3. Valid sessions are authenticated without requiring Bearer token\n4. CSRF validation applies to session-based auth (not Bearer tokens)\n","numLines":140,"startLine":1,"totalLines":140}}}
